title: dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. gracile
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. gracile, also known as the slender jointed clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. gracile include reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tea, or used in tincture form, to harness its medicinal properties.
